behaviors.description: Only files changed in the pull request are analyzed — unchanged files are not scanned.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is most likely at the module handoff between PR-changed-file collection and scan scheduling, not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch this; in-process wiring with fixtures can reproduce wrong file selection. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text only requires correct scope of analyzed files, not real GitHub API/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire changed-file provider + analysis orchestrator + scanner stub together; feed a mixed repo fixture where only a subset is marked changed; assert scanner is invoked only for changed paths and never for unchanged paths.

behaviors.description: Findings are grouped by severity (error, warning, info) in the posted PR comment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: real risk is incorrect handoff between findings collector, grouping logic, and comment renderer/publisher payload. REAL-INFRA: can be caught with in-process stubs for GitHub posting; no real network required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified because behavior text focuses on grouping in comment content, not transport-level GitHub API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run collector->grouping->comment formatting pipeline with mixed severities and a fake PR-comment client; assert generated comment body has correct severity sections and each finding appears under the right section.

behaviors.description: A pull request that touches 200 files completes analysis within the 10-minute Actions timeout.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly depends on GitHub Actions timeout and runtime characteristics, which are OS/process/environment dependent. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing/subprocess behavior can pass while real runner exceeds wall-clock limits. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified by explicit text "within the 10-minute Actions timeout," which requires real execution conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the lint step end-to-end in a CI-like runner against a synthetic PR-sized fixture (200 changed files) using the real linter process and measure wall-clock completion; fail if total runtime exceeds 10 minutes.

behaviors.description: When the linter process crashes on a malformed file, the remaining files are still analyzed and results posted.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior explicitly says "linter process crashes," which is a subprocess/OS boundary. MOCK-HIDE: replacing crash with a normal mock exception can miss true process-exit/signal handling bugs. No LLM dependency or multi-agent sequencing. Rejected lower level: Integration is disqualified by the explicit process-crash wording requiring real subprocess failure semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with a real linter subprocess where one file reliably triggers a crash/abnormal exit; assert pipeline continues processing other files and still emits/posts aggregated results for survivors.

behaviors.description: Duplicate findings on the same line from overlapping rules are deduplicated in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is core dedup logic (keying by file/line/rule overlap), which can be isolated in one function/object. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejected higher level: Integration/System are disqualified because behavior text is about deterministic output deduplication, not module-boundary infra effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call deduplication logic with deterministic findings containing overlapping rules on identical file+line and verify only one canonical entry remains while distinct findings are preserved.
